message:
🐛 Add server-side token revocation with jti-based blocklist (#2451)

Add POST /auth/logout endpoint that revokes the current JWT by adding
its jti (unique token ID) to an in-memory blocklist. The JWTAuth
middleware now checks this blocklist on every request, rejecting
revoked tokens with 401.

Also adds jti claims to all newly issued JWTs and revokes old tokens
during refresh to prevent reuse of rotated credentials.

Security: M6 — previously logout was client-side only, meaning stolen
JWTs remained valid for the full 7-day lifetime.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
